one_time_description: Additional COVID-19 funding received from Indigenous Services Canada for public health measures, income assistance initiatives, and costs related to reopening
economic_notes: Tribe operates a Gas Bar and Convenience Store (financed via Bank of Montreal loan). 677626 Alberta Ltd. (GBE) had net income of $486,665 in 2021 vs loss of $89,895 in 2020. Environmental contamination liability on off-reserve land from former truck stop/gas bar operations. Chevron royalty overpayment judgment of $1,967,506 against the Tribe (under appeal).
notes: Fiscal year 2021 (ended March 31, 2021). Significant COVID-19 additional funding of $7,550,209 from ISC inflated revenues substantially vs prior year. One GBE (677626 Alberta Ltd.) accounted for by modified equity method with large advances of $33.8M and accumulated losses of $30.1M. Two fully consolidated subsidiaries: Kiseputinow Holdings (1996) Ltd. and Asikiw Mostos O'pikinawasiwin Society. Contingent liability of $1,967,506 from Chevron royalty overpayment judgment currently under appeal. Environmental contamination liability unquantified. No schedule of remuneration provided. CMHC replacement reserve of $502,648 included in restricted cash. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($17,543,486) + CMHC subsidies ($461,457) + First Nation Development Funds ($395,905) = $18,400,848; ISET funding ($849,736) also government transfer bringing total to $18,404,943 (note: ISET line treated as government transfer). OSR = total revenue minus total government transfers.
